LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ON LOK, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND THE ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION CLAIM AND DENIED AS TO ALL OTHER CLAIMS. PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT HER PREGNANCY DID NOT CONSTITUTE A DISABILITY. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES WHETHER: 1) PLAINTIFF WAS PROPERLY PERFORMING HER JOB, 2) THERE IS A CAUSAL NEXUS BETWEEN THE LEAVE REQUEST AND THE TERMINATION, AND 3) DEFENDANT DISCHARGED PLAINTIFF FOR A LEGITIMATE REASON. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE OF PRETEXT INCLUDES THE FOLLOWING: DEFENDANT DENIED PLAINTIFF'S FORMAL LEAVE REQUEST, DEFENDANT WAS NOT PLANNING TO HIRE A NEW PERSON TO REPLACE PLAINTIFF, MS. CHANG WAS CHARGED WITH COMMITTING THE SAME WRONGDOING BUT WAS NOT DISCHARGED, AND DEFENDANT AGREED TO MISCHARACTERIZE THE TERMINATION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G)) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL FEDER & ATTY TIM PHILLIPS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF DANIEL FEDER, TEL. NO. 391-9476 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. BROOKE A. BROWN OF EPSTEIN BECKER & GREEN, TEL. NO. 398-3500 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ON LOK, INCORPORATED.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ON LOK, INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - The motion for summary judgment is denied and the alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the second cause of action for disability discrimination claim and denied as to all other claims. Plaintiff concedes that her pregnancy did not constitute a disability. There are triable issues whether: 1) plaintiff was properly performing her job, 2) there is a causal nexus between the leave request and the termination, and 3) defendant discharged plaintiff for a legitimate reason.
Plaintiff's evidence of pretext includes the following: defendant denied plaintiff's formal leave request, defendant was not planning to hire a new person to replace plaintiff, Ms. Chang was charged with committing the same wrongdoing but was not discharged, and defendant agreed to mischaracterize the termination. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g)) Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HK
